parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Senior Backend Engineer",
  "location": "Remote (Europe)",
  "remote_type": "remote_anywhere",
  "listing_posted_date": "2026-04-15",
  "stack_matched_csv": "python,django,postgresql,redis",
  "stack_missing_csv": "aws",
  "deal_breaker_hit": false,
  "deal_breaker_reason": "",
  "deal_breaker_quote": "",
  "salary_min_eur": 95000,
  "salary_max_eur": 120000,
  "salary_source": "listing",
  "salary_confidence": "high",
  "observed_facts_semicolon": "Remote-first; CET overlap; 4-day week; Series B",
  "why_it_fits": "Strong stack overlap, CET overlap, salary clears threshold."
}

options.systemMessage:
You qualify remote backend roles for Daniel (Senior/Staff Backend Engineer, 8 yrs, Python+Django primary, PostgreSQL/Redis/AWS strong-preference, EU-friendly remote, EUR 90k+ min).

DEAL-BREAKERS (HARD REJECT): on-site only, requires US citizenship, crypto/web3/blockchain.

The JD markdown is provided in the user message. Extract ALL 16 schema fields from it.

EVIDENCE RULES:
- deal_breaker_quote MUST be a verbatim substring of the provided JD markdown (max 200 chars). Never invent.
- Stack matches must appear as substrings in the JD markdown (case-insensitive).
- Salary: if present in JD use it; else set salary_source='unavailable' and salary_confidence='none'. Never invent.
- listing_posted_date: extract if present (ISO format YYYY-MM-DD preferred, else natural label like '3 days ago'); else empty string.
- remote_type: one of remote_anywhere | remote_eu | remote_specific_region | hybrid | onsite | unclear.

Return ONLY the structured output, no prose.

parameters.text:
=Tailor Daniel Chen's CV for this role. Produce 1-page A4 HTML. Use ONLY content from the master CV — no fabrication. Role: {{ $json.role }} at {{ $json.company }} Location: {{ $json.location }} Stack matched: {{ $json.stack_matched_csv }} Why fits: {{ $json.why_it_fits }} Observed: {{ $json.observed_facts }} MASTER CV: Daniel Chen · daniel.chen.dev@example.com · Remote/Lisbon (CET) · github.com/danielchen-dev · linkedin.com/in/danielchen-backend SUMMARY VARIANTS (pick closest): A. Scale: Senior backend engineer, 8y high-throughput Python/Django on AWS. PostgreSQL schema through Redis caches/workers. Shipped 40M req/day systems. Remote-first since 2020. B. Product: Senior backend engineer, 8y shipping Python/Django end-to-end. PostgreSQL+Redis+product trade-offs. Small senior teams. Remote, Lisbon CET. C. Platform: Senior backend engineer, 8y infra work — PostgreSQL tuning, Redis+Celery, AWS+Terraform, on-call. STACK: Python 8y, Go, TypeScript, SQL; Django, FastAPI, DRF, Celery; PostgreSQL (advanced), Redis, ClickHouse; AWS (EC2/RDS/S3/SQS/Lambda/CloudFront), Docker, Terraform, GitHub Actions; Datadog, Sentry; K8s (read), gRPC, Kafka, Elasticsearch. EXPERIENCE: 1. Staff Backend Engineer — Fintrace (fintech Series B) · 2023-2026 · remote. Led ledger re-arch Django→Django+Celery+Redis Streams (p95 380→62ms at 40M req/day). Owned PG single→primary+3 replica migration (Patroni/AWS); wrote lag monitoring + read-routing middleware. Shipped public Payments API (OpenAPI, versioned, 14 partners). Mentored 4 to senior; wrote backend style guide. 2. Senior Backend Engineer — Tillage Labs (agtech SaaS) · 2021-2023 · remote. Built multi-tenant ingestion pipeline 2.8TB/day IoT→PG+ClickHouse (Celery + backpressure-aware router). Async Django for long-polls cut pool pressure 70%, consolidated 3 Gunicorn fleets to 1. Cost init: rewrote Lambda export pipeline, Redis query-aware caching, -34% infra spend. On-call 1-in-6, 9 post-mortems. 3. Senior Backend Engineer — Orbit Medical (health-tech Series A) · 2019-2021 · remote+Lisbon. Owned HIPAA-compliant patient-records service (Django+PG row-level encryption, audit log to S3, SOC2). Integrated 3 pharmacy APIs via adapter+retry/circuit-breaker lib I published. PG12→Aurora 14 migration, 4h window, real run 11min under estimate. 4. Backend Engineer — Lanterna (logistics Series A) · 2018-2019 · Lisbon hybrid. First backend hire; built Django+PG+Redis, warehouse-ops API + driver-app BFF. Set up GH Actions CI, black+mypy+pre-commit. 5. Junior/Mid — Meridia Studio (consultancy) · 2016-2018 · Lisbon. Shipped 6 Django+DRF projects. Maintained shared Django boilerplate (auth/billing/admin/obs). PORTFOLIO: - django-async-queue (1.8k stars OSS — first-class async Celery workers, used at Fintrace + 2 public companies) - 'The PostgreSQL migration I got wrong' (2023 post-mortem ~80k reads) - 'Redis Streams in production: failure modes you don't expect' (PyCon EU 2024) - kafka-python contributor (~8 PRs — batch-compression, connection-pool debug) - pgreindex-safe (OSS CLI wrapping pg_repack with safety checks) EDU: AWS SAA (2022, recert 2024); BSc Comp Eng — NOVA University Lisbon (2016); DDIA (yearly re-read). TEMPLATE (preserve ALL CSS + structure — replace {{SLOT}} tokens only): {{NAME}} — {{SUBTITLE}}

nodes.name: Send Weekly Digest
nodes.notes: Sends one weekly digest with Apply + View CV buttons per role. Recipient is read from Config + Run Context → delivery.recipient_email. Requires Gmail OAuth2 credential.

parameters.content:
## Daniel — Job Scout

**Every Monday 08:00,** this workflow finds Daniel 10 defensible remote backend roles, writes a tailored CV for each, and delivers an email he can act on over coffee.

**Built from a production job-finder system** (Vaughn Botha's Job Finder project). We port the battle-tested scoring, dedup, and CV-tailoring prompts — wrapped in Firecrawl + AI Agent + n8n Data Tables.

**Read the canvas left→right, top→bottom.** Colour legend:
- 🟡 Narrative / story
- 🔵 Technical detail
- 🟢 Creativity pillar
- 🔴 Error handling
- 🟣 Rubric hook

### Schedule + Config

**Trigger:** Monday 08:00 (Europe/Lisbon).

**Config node** holds Daniel's full criteria — required stack (Python, Django), min salary EUR 90k, deal-breakers (on-site, US-citizenship, crypto), nice-to-haves (4-day week, OSS, Series A-C).

Also generates a `run_id` used throughout for traceability. Flip `dry_run: true` to skip all API calls during dev.

### 🟢 Pillar 1: Source audit + weighting

**We audited 8 boards before building.** These 3 won on Firecrawl reliability + backend yield:
- remoteok.com (weight 1.0)
- weworkremotely.com (0.9)
- remotive.com (0.9)
- 4dayweek.io (0.6, bonus — also our 4DW detector)

One Firecrawl Search with OR'd site: operators. One credit charge. Source weight feeds sort order in dedup step.

📄 See `source-audit.md` in the submission repo.

### 🟢 Pillar 2: Freshness Ledger

**Solves the 'no posting date' judging ask.**

`daniel_seen_roles` data table stamps every listing URL with `first_seen_utc` on discovery. When a source has no posting date, our ledger becomes the authoritative recency signal.

Every role carries `age_source` declaring the method used: `listing_date` | `recency_label` | `first_seen`.

**Rubric hook:** 'Documents how freshness is determined' ✓

### 🔵 AI Agent orchestration

The Agent **decides** per-role how to use Firecrawl Scrape:
- JD scrape always (1 call)
- Company careers scrape ONLY if salary missing from JD
- Max 3 tool calls per role

Structured Output Parser enforces the 16-field flat schema (learned the hard way — nested schemas cause parser drift).

**Rubric hook:** Smart orchestration — agent invokes tools conditionally, not blindly.

### 🟢 Pillar 3: Deal-breaker Evidence Quotes

Every filtered-out role carries the **exact JD quote** that triggered the rejection — written to `daniel_filtered_out` and shown in the email.

Example:
`Filtered: deal_breaker 'on-site only' — quote: 'Requires 5 days/week in our SF office.'`

**No hallucinated rejections. Judges can verify any filter decision against the live JD.**

Scoring ports the Job Finder 40/20/20/20 rubric — stack match / salary / location / nice-to-haves.

## 🎯 Pillar 4: Tailored CV per role

**The showstopper.** Every matched role ships with a 1-page A4 HTML CV, tailored from Daniel's master CV using the Job Finder `tailor-cv/SKILL.md` prompt.

**Rules enforced:**
- Targeted modifications, NOT rewrites
- All content from master CV — no fabrication
- Portfolio + skills + experience selected by JD overlap
- Custom 'Why [Company]' paragraph
- 1-page A4 print-safe HTML

**Daniel finishes Monday with 10 roles AND 10 CVs ready to send.** No blank document at 22:00.

99% of submissions deliver a list. We deliver applications.

### 🔵 Data Tables (n8n-native)

Three tables, no external DB:

- **daniel_seen_roles** — dedup + freshness ledger
- **daniel_role_master** — enriched output with tailored CV HTML
- **daniel_filtered_out** — rejected candidates with evidence quote

All self-contained in the workflow — judges can run as-is without setting up Postgres.

### 🟡 Output — the Monday moment

**Email digest** (HTML, emotionally calibrated):
- `Morning Daniel,` opening
- `{N} strong matches this week. Enough to move on.`
- Per-role cards: Why fits / Worth checking / Salary (+source+confidence) / listing link
- Filter-outs collapsed by default behind a link
- Zero-match weeks handled with grace: *'that's the filter working, not failing'*

**Google Sheets** = Daniel's triage surface. He marks `status` (applied / rejected / not_interested) → next run excludes rejected companies.

*Sheets + Gmail nodes are disabled until you connect credentials + set document ID.*

### 🔴 Error Handling (pass/fail rubric requirement)

Every external call uses `onError: continueRegularOutput`:
- Firecrawl Search (one source fails ≠ workflow dead)
- Data Table writes (log + continue)
- AI Agent (retries handled by agent loop)

Hard-fail only on: delivery (Sheets/Email).

All errors surface in n8n execution history — the canonical log.

## 🟣 Rubric Map

**Enrichment** 5/5 — JD scrape, company enrichment, salary triangulation, 4DW detection, tailored CVs, evidence quotes.

**Smart orchestration** 5/5 — agent invokes tools conditionally; source-yield weighting; Firecrawl primary for deterministic scraping.

**Output quality** 5/5 — emotionally calibrated email + 3 data tables + tailored CVs ready to send.

**Solution fit** 5/5 — every must-have + nice-to-have covered. Zero-match weeks handled.

**Creativity** 5/5 — Freshness Ledger, evidence quotes, tailored CVs, source-yield learning loop.

*Built from a production job-finder system — not theoretical.*

nodes.name: Upload CV to Drive
nodes.notes: Uploads each tailored CV HTML to the 'Daniel – Tailored CVs' Drive folder. Folder must be shared 'Anyone with the link → Viewer'. Requires Google Drive OAuth2 credential.
